**Morning Market Memo | 23 Jun 2026 | HK/China Pre-Open**

*HK/China Pre-Open briefing | Sources: Eastmoney/21财经 "财经早餐" (23 Jun), Securities Times/STCN, Xinhua, Al Jazeera, Sina/Gelonghui (southbound, property, gold/silver), Eastmoney (CATL), plus TheStreet, NPR/CNBC, Bloomberg, Trading Economics, BBN Times.*

**Setup:** Risk-cautious into the open — Wall Street closed mixed as megacap tech sold off even while US–Iran de-escalation pushed oil to ~3-month lows, and firmer US yields and dollar keep HK liquidity tight. The bigger onshore stories: a fresh US–China escalation (Beijing put MP Materials on its export-control list) and Summer Davos opening in Dalian today.

---

### Global movers (tight)

* **US–Iran roadmap caps oil**: After Monday's first round of talks in Switzerland, mediators say the US and Iran agreed a roadmap to a final deal within 60 days — three working groups (nuclear, sanctions, monitoring) and an MoU covering a full ceasefire (notably Lebanon), lifting the maritime blockade and oil waivers; VP Vance said IAEA inspectors return "this week," and the US Treasury issued a license through 21 Aug for Iranian crude/petrochemical trades. WTI settled −2.3% at $74.08 — softer oil aids HK/China airlines and refiners, a headwind for upstream (CNOOC, PetroChina). [[NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/06/21/g-s1-129222/us-iran-deal-lebanon-israel-strait-hormuz-jd-vance)] [[CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/22/us-iran-roadmap-final-deal-switzerland-talks-lebanon-deconfliction.html)]
* **Fed higher-for-longer; tech-led US drop**: US yields rose Monday (10Y +5bp to 4.51%) and the dollar firmed (DXY 101.0) after this month's Fed hold flagged a possible hike this year. The S&P fell 0.37% to 7,472.79 and the Nasdaq −1.32% as Alphabet (−5%), Amazon, Microsoft and Meta sold off; the Dow rose 0.29% and the Russell 2000 hit 3,000 for the first time. Firmer US rates/dollar cap HK liquidity. [[TheStreet](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-june-22-2026)] [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **SpaceX debuts $20bn AI bond**: SpaceX launched its first investment-grade bond (seeking ≥$20bn, 5–30yr maturities) to refinance an xAI-linked bridge loan and fund its AI/data-center buildout, after bagging IG ratings from Moody's/Fitch/S&P — a marker of the scale of AI-infrastructure financing now hitting credit markets. [[Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-22/spacex-kicks-off-debut-us-dollar-investment-grade-bond-offering)]

**Snapshot:** S&P −0.37% (7,472.79) / Nasdaq −1.32% (26,166.60) / Dow +0.29% · WTI $74.08 (−2.3%), Brent ~$78.1 · spot gold ~$4,194 (−25% from year-high), silver ~$66 · LME copper $13,649/t (+$54), tin $54,185 (+$892) · onshore CNY 6.7763, USD/CNH 6.7774, DXY 101.02 · HSI prior close ~23,769 (−0.65%); HSI futures not retrieved.

---

### China policy & regulation

* **Beijing lists MP Materials, USA Rare Earths (rare earths / US–China)**: MOFCOM on Monday added 10 US firms — rare-earth miner MP Materials, magnet maker USA Rare Earths and aerospace/drone/radar/shipbuilding defence contractors — to its export-control list, barring exports of Chinese dual-use items to them and re-transfers worldwide; the MoF separately barred procurement from 46 firms (Lockheed Martin, Boeing, General Atomics, General Dynamics units). It is tit-for-tat for the Pentagon's early-June listing of ~80 Chinese entities (Alibaba, Baidu, BYD). The move reinforces China's rare-earth leverage — supportive for magnet/rare-earth names (China Northern Rare Earth, JL MAG) and a reminder the security lane stays hot despite the May Trump–Xi truce extension. [[Al Jazeera](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/6/22/china-adds-10-us-firms-including-rare-earth-miner-to-export-control-list)]
* **Summer Davos opens in Dalian today**: The WEF's 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions runs 23–25 June in Dalian on "Scaling Innovation" (1,700+ guests, 90+ countries), agenda dominated by AI and humanoid robots. The MFA confirmed Premier Li Qiang attends 23–24 June — delivering the opening special address and a business roundtable, with visiting PMs from Bangladesh, Guinea, Kazakhstan, South Korea, Mongolia and Montenegro; Li was in Dalian Monday on a Northeast-revitalization inspection. A constructive backdrop for HK/China AI, robotics and clean-energy names. [[Xinhua](https://www.news.cn/politics/leaders/20260622/4ba0f2eaf2aa4e92b0b0884dc100d8a6/c.html)]
* **"Stabilize foreign investment" plan**: At Monday's State Council Information Office briefing, MOFCOM/NDRC/MoF unveiled an Action Plan to Stabilize and Optimize Foreign Investment, letting more foreign institutions use risk tools including treasury-bond futures and run fund advisory business — supportive for the financial-opening theme (brokers, asset managers, CFFEX-linked names). [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **A-share turnover boom — stamp duty +88.8%**: MoF Jan–May data show general public budget revenue of RMB10.05tn (+4% YoY) and securities-transaction stamp duty of RMB126.2bn, +88.8% YoY (total stamp duty +35.8%) — a bullish read for brokers and exchange-volume plays (CITIC Securities, HKEX). [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **Banks curb retail gold/silver leverage (commodities)**: Huaxia, Guangfa and Bank of China on Monday raised margins on agency SGE personal precious-metals deferred contracts — Guangfa and ICBC now at 140%, effectively erasing leverage — to cool speculation after sharp bullion swings; spot gold ~$4,194 and silver ~$66 now sit ~25%/~45% below their year-highs. A regulator-nudged risk-control move that dampens retail precious-metals turnover. [[Sina](https://finance.sina.com.cn/jjxw/2026-06-22/doc-iniehwmp6956579.shtml)]
* **Microstructure: ±10% ST band from 6 Jul**: SSE/SZSE 2026 rule revisions take effect 6 July — main-board ST stocks' daily limit widens to ±10% (from ±5%), post-close fixed-price trading extends to all A-shares/ETFs, and ChiNext gains a market-maker mechanism. More opportunity and tail-risk on ST and extended small-caps. [[STCN](https://www.stcn.com/article/detail/3809580.html)]
* **Online-finance scrutiny**: NFRA, the PBOC and CSRC held a 4th joint liaison meeting on consumer/investor protection, targeting online marketing of financial products, all-in loan-cost disclosure and personal-data governance — mildly negative for aggressive online-lending models. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **Peak-summer power assurance**: China Energy Investment (CHN Energy) said it is going all-out on summer supply, leveraging its integrated coal-power-chemical-transport model to shoulder peak-demand supply-guarantee duties — supportive for energy-supply continuity and coal-chain steadiness. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]

---

### China/HK single-stock catalysts

* **Shenzhen's largest-ever IPO — CR New Energy (001248 CH)**: Subscription launched Monday at RMB10.11/share, ~2.107bn shares (to 2.423bn with the greenshoe), raising ~RMB24.5bn — the biggest IPO in the SZSE's 36-year history and the first red-chip return to the A-share main board. It is 100% held by HK-listed CR Power; a sizeable A-share liquidity drain this week and a read-through for CR Power. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260622/herald/5df9e37b6f15a4875a8da243b568bb5a.html)]
* **CATL sodium-ion timetable (300750 CH)**: At a Monday-evening storage launch, CATL said it will deliver its first sodium-ion battery solutions in China from September, reach GWh-level shipments by end-2026 and start global deliveries from June 2027, citing ~15,000-cycle life, 20-year system life and costs 10–20% below the industry average (€650m/+40GWh at Fuding, a planned +160GWh at Jining). A concrete commercialization catalyst for the sodium/storage chain. [[Eastmoney](https://finance.eastmoney.com/a/202606223778423977.html)]
* **Haitian buyback**: Foshan Haitian Flavouring approved a RMB1–2bn A-share buyback at ≤RMB53/share, ≥70% for cancellation — shareholder-friendly for consumer staples and a positive read for its H-line. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **Strong H1 guidance (chips & petchem)**: Satellite Chemical guided H1 net to RMB6.0–7.0bn (+119% to +155% YoY); chip-test maker Chuangchun Tech RMB0.90–1.00bn (+111% to +134%); Beijing Junzheng's Q1 compute-chip revenue rose ~50%. Constructive for petrochemical and chip-equipment sentiment. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **Halts & risk flags**: CSSC Special Gas (688146 CH) is suspended from today's open after a 388.77% run (11 May–22 Jun) pending verification; Beidahuang takes a ~RMB1.41bn back-tax hit to FY26 profit; Xinsai Shares is under CSRC investigation for disclosure breaches (Saiwu Technology denied similar rumors). Caution on extended small/mid-caps. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]

---

### China tech & AI; sector & flow signals

* **MIIT humanoid-robot standards**: The MIIT opened consultation on humanoid-robot industry standards (substation and appliance-manufacturing uses) plus after-sales and interconnect specs — supportive for the embodied-AI/robotics chain, timely into Summer Davos's robot focus. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **"Computing-power" minor-metals squeeze (commodities)**: Per CLS, AI-demand minor metals are spiking on tight supply — tin has run ~RMB300k→400k/t since November, tantalum ingot +158% since end-2025 and indium +~60% YTD; LME tin jumped $892 to $54,185 on Monday. A tailwind for small-metals miners and processors. [[21财经](https://www.21jingji.com/article/20260623/herald/ea60133b89e55f67aaa2a0dcd0363895.html)]
* **Shenzhen resale heats up**: Dragon Boat-holiday (19–21 Jun) second-hand home signings at Beike's Shenzhen partner stores rose 28% YoY — a near-6-year high — on cumulative policy easing and a full viewing window, with both first-time and upgrade demand active. A constructive demand-proxy for HK/China property. [[Sina](https://finance.sina.cn/2026-06-22/detail-iniehmvv9320421.d.html?vt=4)]
* **Southbound rotates out of megacaps**: Southbound net-sold HK$5.82bn on Monday, rotating out of internet megacaps — Alibaba (−HK$1.18bn; 9-day −HK$15.5bn) and Tencent (−HK$1.15bn; 5-day −HK$6.7bn) — into the Kingboard/PCB and domestic-chip complex: Kingboard Laminates (10-day +HK$12.7bn), Kingboard Holdings (5-day +HK$8.8bn), SMIC (7-day +HK$4.0bn) and Hua Hong (4-day +HK$1.8bn). [[Sina/Gelonghui](https://finance.sina.com.cn/stock/bxjj/2026-06-22/doc-iniehscq0229881.shtml)]

US close, Fed/Treasuries, Iran, oil/metals, SpaceX and the HSI level came from same-run web searches (TheStreet, NPR/CNBC, Bloomberg, Trading Economics, BBN Times). Southbound total net sell is HK$5.82bn (Gelonghui); STCN's −HK$0.38bn is the narrower active-leaderboard subset. Not separately retrieved: a live HSI futures level and an intraday USD/CNH (used the 22 Jun 18:00 CNH 6.7774). LPR (held) and the weekly A-share unlock total were not re-sourced this run and are omitted.*
